A Lighthouse Score is a comprehensive performance metric generated by Google’s open-source tool, Lighthouse, which analyzes web pages and provides scores across multiple categories to help developers improve their websites’ quality.

Tech Stack

This is a scan of https://btebresultszone.com/results to determine what technologies it was made with. From analytics tools, to hosting provider, frameworks and more, here's what we found.

JavaScript frameworks

React
React is an open-source JavaScript library for building user interfaces or UI components.
AMP
AMP, originally created by Google, is an open-source HTML framework developed by the AMP open-source Project. AMP is designed to help webpages load faster.
Next.js
Next.js is a React framework for developing single page Javascript applications.

Web frameworks

Next.js
Next.js is a React framework for developing single page Javascript applications.

Web servers

Next.js
Next.js is a React framework for developing single page Javascript applications.

Static site generator

Next.js
Next.js is a React framework for developing single page Javascript applications.

Analytics

Cloudflare Browser Insights
Cloudflare Browser Insights is a tool that measures the performance of websites from the perspective of users.
Microsoft Clarity
Microsoft's Clarity is a analytics tool which provides website usage statistics, session recording, and heatmaps.
Google Analytics
Google Analytics is a free web analytics service that tracks and reports website traffic.

RUM

Cloudflare Browser Insights
Cloudflare Browser Insights is a tool that measures the performance of websites from the perspective of users.

Tag managers

Google Tag Manager
Google Tag Manager is a tag management system (TMS) that allows you to quickly and easily update measurement codes and related code fragments collectively known as tags on your website or mobile app.

Advertising

Google AdSense
Google AdSense is a program run by Google through which website publishers serve advertisements that are targeted to the site content and audience.
DoubleClick Floodlight

Performance

Priority Hints
Priority Hints exposes a mechanism for developers to signal a relative priority for browsers to consider when fetching resources.

Font scripts

Lucide
Lucide is an open-source icon library that provides 1000+ vector (svg) files for displaying icons and symbols in digital and non-digital projects.

CDN

Cloudflare
Cloudflare is a web-infrastructure and website-security company, providing content-delivery-network services, DDoS mitigation, Internet security, and distributed domain-name-server services.

Miscellaneous

Webpack
Webpack is an open-source JavaScript module bundler.
PWA
Progressive Web Apps (PWAs) are web apps built and enhanced with modern APIs to deliver enhanced capabilities, reliability, and installability while reaching anyone, anywhere, on any device, all with a single codebase.
Open Graph
Open Graph is a protocol that is used to integrate any web page into the social graph.
HTTP/3
HTTP/3 is the third major version of the Hypertext Transfer Protocol used to exchange information on the World Wide Web.

A visual representation of this website's structure, displayed as an interactive tree diagram. Each node represents a page, with lines connecting parent pages to their children. Large sections can be collapsed and expanded by clicking on nodes with many children.
